rationale: Article VIII, §7-a governs use of motor vehicle registration and motor fuel tax revenues for public roadways and schools. It is unrelated to the residence homestead exemption under Tax Code §11.13(b).
quote: all net revenues remaining after payment of all refunds allowed by law and expenses of collection derived from motor vehicle registration fees, and all taxes, except gross production and ad valorem taxes, on motor fuels and lubricants used to propel motor vehicles over public roadways, shall be used for the sole purpose of acquiring rights-of-way, constructing, maintaining, and policing such public roadways
_note: No conflict; the cited constitutional provision addresses motor fuel/vehicle tax revenue allocation, not property tax homestead exemptions.
